req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 3
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three test axes: (1) file format allow-list (PDF/PNG/JPG vs everything else), (2) file size boundary (<=10 MB accepted, >10 MB rejected with 413), and (3) side-effects (document stored, virus scan queued, webhook emitted). I derive partitions EP: valid-format vs invalid-format; BVA: exactly 10 MB (upper boundary), 10 MB+1 byte (over boundary); NEG: unsupported MIME type (415); DT: combination of size+format for reject path; STC: upload transitions state from 'pending' to 'stored+scan_queued'. Five tests cover positive (PDF under limit), boundary-exact (10 MB PDF), boundary-over (10 MB+1), negative-mime (TXT), and negative-size (>10 MB JPG) cases, satisfying the minimum three-test requirement while providing comprehensive coverage.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_stores_document_and_emits_webhook
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
HR app is reachable at BASE_URL
Webhook receiver mock is running and accessible
actions: Create an in-memory PDF file of 1 MB
POST the file to /documents/upload with valid auth header
Poll the webhook receiver for the upload event
expected: Response body indicates virus_scan status is 'queued'
Webhook receiver received exactly one event with event_type='document.uploaded'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_stores_document_and_emits_webhook(
    auth_session,        # <fixture>: requests.Session with auth headers
    base_url,            # <fixture>: str, e.g. "http://hr-app"
    webhook_receiver,    # <fixture>: mock server capturing webhook calls
    make_pdf_bytes,      # <helper_fn>(size_bytes) -> bytes
):
    pdf_bytes = make_pdf_bytes(1 * 1024 * 1024)  # 1 MB
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.pdf", io.BytesIO(pdf_bytes), "application/pdf")}

    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan") == "queued", "Virus scan not queued"

    events = webhook_receiver.get_events(timeout=5)
    upload_events = [e for e in events if e.get("event_type") == "document.uploaded"]
    assert len(upload_events) == 1, f"Expected 1 webhook event, got {len(upload_events)}"
    assert upload_events[0]["document_id"] == body["document_id"]

tests.name: test_upload_pdf_exactly_10mb_is_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
HR app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Create an in-memory PDF file of exactly 10485760 bytes (10 MB)
POST the file to /documents/upload with valid auth header
expected: File is not rejected due to size
trace: R-WEB-079: files up to 10 MB must be accepted
BVA upper boundary at exactly 10 MB
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

MAX_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # 10485760 bytes

def test_upload_pdf_exactly_10mb_is_accepted(
    auth_session,      # <fixture>
    base_url,          # <fixture>
    make_pdf_bytes,    # <helper_fn>(size_bytes) -> bytes
):
    pdf_bytes = make_pdf_bytes(MAX_SIZE)
    assert len(pdf_bytes) == MAX_SIZE, "Fixture must produce exact size"

    files = {"file": ("large.pdf", io.BytesIO(pdf_bytes), "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected acceptance at 10 MB boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body

tests.name: test_upload_pdf_one_byte_over_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
HR app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Create an in-memory PDF file of 10485761 bytes (10 MB + 1 byte)
POST the file to /documents/upload with valid auth header
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing size limit
trace: R-WEB-079: files outside 10 MB must be rejected
R-WEB-079: error pathway 413 when size > 10 MB
BVA one byte above upper boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

OVER_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1  # 10485761 bytes

def test_upload_pdf_one_byte_over_10mb_returns_413(
    auth_session,      # <fixture>
    base_url,          # <fixture>
    make_pdf_bytes,    # <helper_fn>(size_bytes) -> bytes
):
    pdf_bytes = make_pdf_bytes(OVER_SIZE)
    assert len(pdf_bytes) == OVER_SIZE, "Fixture must produce exact size"

    files = {"file": ("toolarge.pdf", io.BytesIO(pdf_bytes), "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code == 413, (
        f"Expected 413 for oversized file, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Error payload missing"

tests.name: test_upload_unsupported_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
HR app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Create a small plain-text file (text/plain, not in allow-list)
POST the file to /documents/upload with valid auth header
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing unsupported media type
trace: R-WEB-079: only PDF, PNG, JPG are accepted
R-WEB-079: error pathway 415 when MIME not in allow-list
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_unsupported_mime_type_returns_415(
    auth_session,    # <fixture>
    base_url,        # <fixture>
):
    txt_content = b"This is a plain text document that should be rejected."
    files = {"file": ("notes.txt", io.BytesIO(txt_content), "text/plain")}

    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code == 415, (
        f"Expected 415 for unsupported MIME type, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "unsupported" in error_text or "media" in error_text or "type" in error_text, (
        f"Error message does not reference MIME issue: {body}"
    )

tests.name: test_upload_jpg_png_valid_formats_are_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
HR app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Create a minimal valid JPG file under 1 MB
Create a minimal valid PNG file under 1 MB
expected: Both response bodies contain a document_id
trace: R-WEB-079: accepted formats include PNG and JPG
R-WEB-079: document stored for each valid format
DT: decision table row — valid size AND valid MIME => accept
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

@pytest.mark.parametrize("filename,mime,make_fn", [
    ("photo.jpg", "image/jpeg", "make_jpg_bytes"),
    ("scan.png",  "image/png",  "make_png_bytes"),
])
def test_upload_jpg_png_valid_formats_are_accepted(
    filename, mime, make_fn,
    auth_session,      # <fixture>
    base_url,          # <fixture>
    request,           # pytest built-in for dynamic fixture lookup
):
    make_image = request.getfixturevalue(make_fn)  # <helper_fn>(size_bytes)->bytes
    img_bytes = make_image(512 * 1024)  # 512 KB

    files = {"file": (filename, io.BytesIO(img_bytes), mime)}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"[{mime}] Expected 2xx, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, f"[{mime}] document_id missing"
    assert body.get("virus_scan") == "queued", f"[{mime}] virus scan not queued"
